title: Belgium vs Egypt: World Cup 2026 Prediction | Squawka
original_text:
Belgium vs Egypt kicks off on Monday, June 15 at 3:00 PM ET / 2:00 PM CT / 12:00 PM PT at Lumen Field in Seattle, a Group G opener that pits one of Europe’s deepest squads against an Egypt side built around Mohamed Salah.
Our Belgium vs Egypt prediction starts with the gap in attacking firepower. Belgium arrive with Kevin De Bruyne, Jérémy Doku and a settled spine, while Egypt lean heavily on Salah to carry the load against a stronger opponent. Both nations want a fast start in a group that also holds Iran and New Zealand.
bet365 prices Belgium at -150 to win, with Egypt out at +400 and the draw at +280. That line reads as a clear favorite who still has to break down a disciplined African side, and you can track the full market on the World Cup 2026 hub and our World Cup outright odds page.
Market consensus: Kalshi traders price Belgium at 61%, the draw at 24%, and Egypt at 17%. Full breakdown below.
Belgium vs Egypt: Best Bets & Predictions
Our approach to this opener leans on Belgium’s edge in quality without overpaying for a blowout.

Group G — 2026 FIFA World Cup
The table below reflects the December draw, not a played group, so every team starts on zero. Belgium are the seeds and the favorites to top the group ahead of Egypt, Iran and New Zealand.
TeamPosGPWDLGFGAGDPts
Belgium 1 0
Egypt 2 0
Iran 3 0
New Zealand 4 0
Potential Match-Winners
Jérémy Doku (Belgium): The winger scored five goals and laid on five assists in 29 Premier League appearances for Manchester City this season, giving Belgium a direct runner to stretch Egypt’s defense.
Kevin De Bruyne (Belgium): Now at Napoli, he managed five goals and two assists across 18 Serie A games this season and remains Belgium’s chief creator from midfield.
Mohamed Salah (Egypt): Egypt’s talisman shared the Premier League Golden Boot with 23 goals and added 13 assists for Liverpool this season, and he is the one player who can swing this game on his own.
Omar Marmoush (Egypt): The Manchester City forward chipped in three goals and three assists in 21 Premier League outings this season, offering Egypt a second pace threat alongside Salah.
Head-to-Head
Egypt hold the upper hand in this rivalry, winning three of the four meetings, including a 2-1 friendly success in 2022. All four games have been friendlies, so there is no competitive history to read into here.
DateScoreCompetition
Nov 18, 2022 Egypt 2-1 Belgium Friendly
Jun 6, 2018 Belgium 3-0 Egypt Friendly
Feb 9, 2005 Egypt 4-0 Belgium Friendly
Mar 30, 1999 Belgium 0-1 Egypt Friendly
Form Coming Into the Tournament
Belgium reached the finals through European qualifying and arrive with most of their first-choice spine intact. De Bruyne, Doku and Romelu Lukaku give the side a familiar attacking shape, and the squad blends experienced heads with younger legs across the pitch.
Egypt qualified comfortably from African qualifying, anchored by Salah’s goals and a settled defensive group. The Pharaohs are back at a World Cup with a clear plan: stay compact, frustrate stronger opponents, and let Salah punish any opening. They will fancy their chances of springing a surprise in a winnable group.
Belgium have the better players in almost every area, and an opening group game against a side likely to sit deep points to a controlled win rather than a goal rush. We will take Belgium to win at -150 with bet365 as the anchor, with the Doku anytime scorer as a value add. For more of our weekend picks, see our soccer predictions, and you can compare this one with our Mexico vs South Africa preview for the tournament opener. Kick-off is Monday, June 15 at 3:00 PM ET at Lumen Field in Seattle.
Odds correct at the time of writing. Please gamble responsibly.
